items.docs: Returns available Futures markets for the given season. Does not include line movement.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: Returns the markets of all available types (e.g. Player Props, Team Props) and available outcomes for a given BettingEventID.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: marketId
params.description: The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets.

items.docs: Returns full line movement for a given BettingMarket. Due to the sheer size of the output and the level of detail, it is intended for historical data purposes and not for the most up-to-the-second lines.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

params.name: marketTypeID
params.description: The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for Game Lines, 2 for Player Props, 3 for Team Props, 6 for Game Props

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: Returns Markets and available outcomes for a given event and market type requested. A lighter call than by BettingEventID as it only includes markets tagged with the specific MarketType, a full list of which is available for each sport in its Betting Metadata endpoint.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: scoreid
params.description: The ScoreID of the desired game/score for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: Returns the markets of all available types (e.g. Player Props, Team Props) and available outcomes for a given GameID.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

items.docs: Returns the list of MarketTypes, BetTypes, PeriodTypes, OutcomeTypes, and ResultTypes to map the IDs to descriptive names. Also includes a list of the MarketType, BetType & PeriodType combinations which we will have resulting for.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

items.docs: Returns a list for mapping SportsbookID to the Sportsbook name.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: scoreId
params.description: The ScoreID of the desired game to get Betting Market Splits for

items.docs: List of Money and Bet Percentage splits for each market and their respective outcome types available in this game. This specific endpoint will return current splits for each available market and no line movement.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: marketId
params.description: The BettingMarketID of the market you would like to get splits for. Note that markets we do not have split information on will return an empty response.

items.docs: List of Money and Bet Percentage splits for each outcome type available in this market. This specific endpoint will return the movement from this market as well as the most recent.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: marketId
params.description: The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets.

items.docs: Provide a market ID that supports resulting (i.e. has a ResultType) and this endpoint will return a result: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: scoreid
params.description: The unique ScoreID of the game in question.

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: This works in the same way as Betting Markets by Market Type but is prefiltered to the Player Props type only. Ideal if your application will only ever require Player Props, but if you also want Team Props etc. it is recommended to use the by Market Type endpoint.

info.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds - by Week [Sportsbook Group]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: season
params.description:
Year of the season, with optional season type.
Examples: 2018, 2018POST, etc.

params.name: week
params.description:
The week of the scores (games).
Examples: 1, 2, etc.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns the full-game and non-full-game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games in a given week and season. Only returns the most recently seen odds, not inclusive of line movement. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: scoreid
params.description: The ScoreID of an NFL score (game). ScoreIDs can be found in the Scores API. Valid entries are 16654 or 16667

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns the game odds (for games in a given week and season. In this endpoint both full-game and partial-game odds are included. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games in a given week and season. Only returns the most recently seen odds, not inclusive of line movement. As this is in-game, it will only return results while the game is in progress. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.description: Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

params.name: season
params.description:
Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.

params.name: week
params.description: Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

items.docs: Delivers all offensive stats within the 5-yard line of the defensive team for all players in a given season or week.

info.name: Player Season Red Zone Stats (Inside Five)

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: season
params.description:
Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.

items.docs: Delivers all offensive stats within the 5-yard line of the defensive team for all players in a given season (i.e. the season total, not each individual game record.)

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: season
params.description: Season to get games from. Example 2019POST, 2020

params.name: playerid
params.description: Unique SportsDataIO Player ID. Example:17920.

params.name: numberofgames
params.description: How many games to return. Example all, 10, 25

items.docs: Specify a season, a player, and number of games (either an integer or all) to see all of their box score logs. Refreshed after their most recent game is complete.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: scoreid
params.description: The ScoreID of the game. Possible values include: 16247, 16245, etc.

items.docs: Each invididual play, its type and outcome, complete with player and team stats down to the play level, delivered live in real-time, called by team.

params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: numberofplays
params.description: The number of plays to progress in this NFL live game simulation. Example entries are 0, 1, 2, 3, 150, 200, etc.

items.docs: Gets simulated live play-by-play of NFL games, covering the Conference Championship games on January 21, 2018.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

Year of the season and the season type. If no season type is provided, then the default is regular season.
Examples: 2015REG, 2015PRE, 2015POST.

params.name: week
params.description: Week of the season. Valid values are as follows: Preseason 0 to 4, Regular Season 1 to 17, Postseason 1 to 4. Example: 1

params.name: minutes
params.description:
Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are:
1 or 2.

items.docs: This method returns all play-by-plays for a given season and week, but only returnsplays that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. by definition this is a live endpoint, not final.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of a NFL game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14620 or 16905
